title: Thermal Management Challenges in Chiplet-based Architectures
gist: A SemiWiki forum post frames thermal density and heat dissipation as emerging critical bottlenecks for chiplet-based HPC/AI architectures, raising open questions on CTE matching of TIM/substrates, the limits of traditional TIM in 3D stacking, the rise of microfluidic/immersion cooling, and the lack of standards for multi-die thermal-electrical co-simulation.

insights.claim: As chiplet architectures become common in HPC and AI chips, thermal density and heat dissipation have become critical bottlenecks for performance scaling, introducing thermal challenges distinct from monolithic integration.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Frames thermal management as the gating constraint on chiplet performance scaling, a demand driver for advanced packaging and cooling suppliers.
insights.quote: thermal density and heat dissipation have become critical bottlenecks for performance scaling
insights.locator: As Chiplet architectures become increasingly common

insights.claim: In 2.5D/3D stacking, the high heat flux of compute dies creates extreme thermal gradients near adjacent I/O or cache dies, placing stringent requirements on the CTE (coefficient of thermal expansion) matching of packaging materials such as TIM and substrates.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: CTE-matched materials become a reliability chokepoint in stacked dies; a watch item for packaging-material and substrate vendors.
insights.quote: the high heat flux of compute dies often creates extreme thermal gradients near adjacent I/O or cache dies

insights.claim: In 3D stacking with TSVs, shrinking die-to-die contact area may render traditional TIM insufficient for heat dissipation, raising the question of whether the industry shifts from material optimization toward microfluidic or immersion cooling.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Signals a potential inflection from thermal-interface-material solutions toward micro-scale/liquid cooling in 3D-stacked dies.
insights.quote: Is the focus more on optimizing the materials themselves, or are we shifting toward microfluidic cooling or immersion solutions?

insights.claim: Accurately predicting transient thermal responses in multi-die designs requires thermal-electrical co-simulation, but there is an open gap around industry-recognized standards for efficient modeling.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: A standards gap in multi-die thermal simulation is an opportunity for EDA/co-simulation tool vendors.
insights.quote: Are there any industry-recognized standards for efficient modeling?
